methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Radian Forge is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jeff Yeager that has raised $3M+. The target operates within a sector containing 9 comparable peers—1 public and 8 private—including companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The stage-progression method is applicable and drives the valuation estimate. Using total capital raised of $3M as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, this approach yields $7.5M. The peer-median method is not applicable; although 11 same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, the comparable set lacks sufficient depth at matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier to support a statistically reliable median. A cross-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given robotics-specific dynamics.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $6M to $9M, centered on a median of $8M. This range reflects a single applicable method and carries material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation multiples and the representativeness of the step-up multiplier to this company's specific risk profile. Key unknowns include Radian Forge's revenue trajectory, technology differentiation within the crowded robotics space, and the sustainability of typical seed-to-next-stage valuation progression in a potentially correcting frontier-tech market.
